tips: The test covers vocabulary, grammar, and reading comprehension.
Questions span 6 difficulty levels: A1 (Beginner) to C2 (Proficiency).
Answer honestly for the most accurate result.
Each question has 4 options, and only one is correct.
Your result will include personalized learning tips.

idle.assessDescription: Take a quick test (CEFR A1-C2) to get personalized learning recommendations.

result.balanced: Balanced performance across all areas. Keep practicing to strengthen each skill!

weakTips.vocabulary: Try learning 5-10 new words daily with flashcards. Read graded readers at your level and note unfamiliar words in context.
weakTips.grammar: Review core grammar rules (tenses, articles, prepositions). Practice with fill-in-the-blank exercises and pay attention to patterns when reading.
weakTips.reading: Start with short texts (news headlines, short stories) and gradually increase length. Practice skimming for main ideas and scanning for details.

A1.label: Beginner
A1.summary: You can understand and use basic everyday expressions.
A1.canDo: Introduce yourself, ask simple questions about personal details, and interact in a simple way if the other person speaks slowly.
A1.tip: Focus on basic vocabulary, simple sentence patterns, and daily conversation phrases.

A2.label: Elementary
A2.summary: You can communicate in simple, routine tasks on familiar topics.
A2.canDo: Describe your background, immediate environment, and matters of immediate need in simple terms.
A2.tip: Expand your vocabulary around daily topics and practice simple past and future tenses.

B1.label: Intermediate
B1.summary: You can deal with most situations likely to arise while travelling.
B1.canDo: Describe experiences, events, dreams, and ambitions. Give reasons and explanations for opinions and plans.
B1.tip: Practice reading short articles, watching English media with subtitles, and writing paragraphs.

B2.label: Upper Intermediate
B2.summary: You can interact with a degree of fluency with native speakers.
B2.canDo: Understand the main ideas of complex text on concrete and abstract topics. Produce clear, detailed text on a wide range of subjects.
B2.tip: Read longer articles, watch English content without subtitles, and practice expressing opinions on complex topics.

C1.label: Advanced
C1.summary: You can use English flexibly and effectively for social, academic, and professional purposes.
C1.canDo: Understand demanding, longer texts and recognize implicit meaning. Express ideas fluently and spontaneously without much searching for expressions.
C1.tip: Focus on nuance, idiomatic expressions, and academic or professional writing skills.

C2.label: Proficiency
C2.summary: You can understand virtually everything heard or read with ease.
C2.canDo: Summarize information from different spoken and written sources. Express yourself spontaneously, very fluently, and precisely.
C2.tip: Maintain your level through extensive reading, professional writing, and engaging with complex content.
